docs.title: CONTROL — Lua Scripting Engine for Age of Empires II
docs.text: CONTROL is a Lua scripting engine that communicates directly with Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition. It reads game state and sends commands without pixel scanning or mouse/keyboard simulation.

docs.title: What is it?
docs.text:
The engine talks directly to the game, allowing scripters to:

  • Read game state (units, resources, players, map data)
  • Send commands (train units, move armies, research technologies)
  • Render custom overlays (HUD elements, minimap markers, world-space indicators)
  • Configure modules via a built-in settings interface

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Description CONTROL Launcher Attaches the engine to the game Extensive API Commands, facts, render functions, and strategic components Custom Rendering Draw shapes and text in screen, world, or minimap space Menu Settings Add checkboxes, sliders, dropdowns, keybinds, and color pickers Multi-file Projects Use require() for modular Lua code (depth limit: 3) IPC for ML Named pipes + JSON for external agents (e.g. Python) Encrypted Modules Precompiled .module files for code protection VS Code Plugin Planned — autocomplete and suggestions for endpoints

docs.title: Target Audience
docs.text:
• Lua scripters — Build bots, overlays, or automation
  • AI/ML developers — Connect external agents via IPC
  • AoE2 modders — Create custom in-game tools

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition (Steam)
  • Basic Lua familiarity is helpful but not required
  • Windows

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started — Installation and first script
  • Quick Example — Minimal working module
  • Game API — Commands and facts reference
  • Render API — Drawing overlays

docs.title: AI Agent Reference
docs.text:
This document provides complete information about the CONTROL Lua scripting engine for Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition. Copy-paste this file into a coding agent (Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT, etc.) to help users with limited Lua knowledge quickly build modules.

Target: Coding agents assisting users to write CONTROL Lua modules. The agent should use this as the sole source of truth for engine behavior and API.

docs.title: 1. Overview
docs.text:
CONTROL is a Lua scripting engine that communicates directly with Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition. It reads game state and sends commands without pixel scanning or mouse/keyboard simulation.

  • Platform: Windows, single-player only (multiplayer disabled)
  • Config location: %appdata%\CONTROL\AoE2Control\
  • Module folder: %appdata%\CONTROL\AoE2Control\modules\
  • Entry point: {moduleName}.main.lua or {moduleName}.main.module (encrypted)

docs.title: Folder Convention
docs.text:
modules/
└── {moduleName}/
    ├── {moduleName}.main.lua   ← Entry point (required)
    └── utils/
        └── logger.lua          ← Optional submodules

The folder name must match the module name. The engine searches recursively for *.main.lua or *.main.module up to depth 3.

docs.title: require Behavior
docs.text:
• require("utils.logger") → loads utils/logger.lua (relative to project root)
  • Dot notation becomes path separators
  • Depth limit: 3 — require("a.b.c.d") fails
  • No .. — directory traversal forbidden
  • Caching: Modules are cached; second require returns cached result
  • Sandbox: load, loadfile, dofile, module, collectgarbage are removed

docs.title: Example Submodule
docs.text:
-- utils/logger.lua
local logger = {}

function logger.info(message)
    Log("[Bot] " .. message)
end

return logger

docs.title: 3. Lifecycle Callbacks
docs.text:
All callbacks are optional. Implement only what you need.

Callback When Use for Load Once when module is selected Settings.Add* only. Do NOT call game API. Init When game is ready, world changed, module reloaded, or AI enabled One-time setup (e.g. IPC.StartServer, ChatLocal) Update At configurable interval (default 1.0s) while game runs Main game logic — commands, decisions Render Every frame while game runs Drawing overlays End Once when game ends Cleanup, receives hasWon (boolean)

Order: Load → Init → (Update ↔ Render loop) → End

Important: Game API (commands, facts, render) must be called only from Init, Update, or Render. Calling before the game starts logs an error.

docs.title: 4. Commands (Game API — Actions)
docs.text:
Call from Init, Update, or Render when the game is active.

Function Parameters Description Complexity Log (message) Write to log window Not complex SetGameSpeedMultiplier (multiplier) Set game speed (e.g. 1.5, 30) Not complex SetCameraPosition (position) Move camera; position is Vector2 Not complex ChatLocal (message) Send message to local chat Not complex TrainUnit (trainSources, unitId, amount?) Train units. trainSources = table of UnitObjectType (buildings), unitId = unit type, amount defaults to 1 Low UnitsTargetObject (units, target) Order units to attack/target object Low UnitsBuildStructure (builders, structureId, position) Order builders to construct Low UnitsMove (units, position) Move units to Vector3 position Low EnableScouting () Enable auto-scouting; returns boolean Low ResearchTechnology (researchSources, technology) Research tech at specified buildings Low DeleteUnit (unit) Delete a unit Low DestroyBuilding (building) Destroy a building Low SetGatherPoint (buildings, targetPosition) Set gather point for buildings Low RingTownBell (building, isCallingIn) Ring town bell Low SendBackToWork (building) Send garrisoned units back to work Low SendAllBackToWork (building) Send all garrisoned units back to work Low SetUnitStanceAutoScout (units) Set units to auto-scout Low SetUnitStancePatrol (units, targetPosition) Set units to patrol Low SetUnitStanceGuard (units, targetObject) Set units to guard object Low SetUnitStanceFollow (units, targetObject) Set units to follow object Low SetUnitStanceAttackMove (units, targetPosition) Set units to attack-move Low SetUnitStanceGarrison (units, targetObject) Garrison units in building Low SetUnitStanceUngarrison (sourceObjects, unit) Ungarrison unit Low SetUnitStanceSeekShelter (units) Set units to seek shelter Low SetUnitCombatStance (units, stance) Set combat stance (UnitCombatStance enum) Low

TrainUnit example: 

TrainUnit({UnitObjectType.TOWN_CENTER_FEUDAL_AGE}, UnitObjectType.VILLAGER_MALE, 1)

docs.title: 5. Facts (Game API — Read State)
docs.text:
Call from Init, Update, or Render when the game is active.

Function Parameters Returns Description GetFact (factId, parameter?) number Fact value (population, resources, etc.). parameter often 0 GetUnitTypeCount (unitId) number Count of unit type for local player GetAttribute (attribute) number Player attribute (PlayerAttribute enum) CanAfford (unitId, isBuilding?) boolean Can local player afford CanResearch (technology) boolean Can research technology IsTechnologyResearched (technology) boolean Is technology researched GetObjectsByType (unitType) Table of Object Objects of specific type GetObjectsByTypes (unitTypes) Table of Object Objects matching any type GetObjectsByClass (unitClass) Table of Object Objects of class (e.g. UnitClass.VILLAGER) GetGameTime () number Current game time (seconds) GetLocalPlayer () Player Local player object GetPlayerById (id) Player Player by ID GetPlayerCount () number Number of players IsEnemyPlayer (player) boolean Is player an enemy GetObjectById (id) Object Object by ID GetVictoryCondition () VictoryCondition Current victory condition GetVictoryPlayer () Player? Winning player (if game ended)

GetFact example: 

local pop = GetFact(FactId.POPULATION, 0)
local food = GetFact(FactId.FOOD_AMOUNT, 0)

docs.title: 6. Render API
docs.text: Use in Render(). Three coordinate spaces: Screen (HUD), World (map), Minimap.

docs.title: Base (Screen Space)
docs.text: Function Parameters GetScreenSize () → Vector2 IsOnScreen (position) RenderText (text, position, size, color, center, border?) RenderLine (from, to, color, thickness) RenderCircle (position, radius, color, thickness, segments?) RenderCircleFilled (position, radius, color, segments?) RenderRect (from, to, color, rounding, roundingCornersFlags, thickness) RenderRectFilled (from, to, color, rounding, roundingCornersFlags)

docs.title: World / Minimap
docs.text: Function Parameters IsWorldPosOnScreen (worldPos) WorldToScreen (worldPos) → Vector2 WorldToMinimap (worldPos) → Vector2 GetZoom () GetCameraPosition () → Vector2 RenderWorldLine (from, to, color, thickness?) RenderWorldRect (worldPos, width, height, color, thickness?) RenderWorldRectFilled (worldPos, width, height, color) RenderWorldCircle (worldPos, radius, color, thickness?, segments?) RenderWorldCircleFilled (worldPos, radius, color, segments?) RenderWorldText (text, worldPos, size, color, center?, border?) RenderObjectBounds (object, color, thickness?) RenderObjectBoundsFilled (object, color) — exactly 2 params RenderMinimapDot (worldPos, radius, color) RenderMinimapLine (worldPosFrom, worldPosTo, thickness, color) RenderMinimapRect (worldPos, width, height, color, thickness) RenderMinimapRectFilled (worldPos, width, height, color)

docs.title: 7. Settings API
docs.text:
Add functions — call only from Load():

Function Parameters Settings.AddBool (key, default) Settings.AddInt (key, default, min, max) Settings.AddFloat (key, default, min, max) Settings.AddDropdown (key, default, options) — options = table of strings Settings.AddKeybind (key, defaultVkCode) — use Key.Add, Key.F, Key.SPACE, etc. Settings.AddColor (key, defaultColor) Settings.AddTooltip (key, tooltip)

Get functions — call from anywhere:

Function Returns Settings.GetBool (key, default?) → boolean Settings.GetInt (key, default?) → number Settings.GetFloat (key, default?) → number Settings.GetString (key, default?) → string Settings.GetKeybind (key, defaultVkCode?) → number Settings.GetColor (key, defaultColor) → Color

IsKeyPressed: IsKeyPressed(vkCode) → boolean. Use with Settings.GetKeybind for hotkeys.

docs.title: 8. IPC API
docs.text:
For external processes (e.g. Python ML agents) via named pipes and JSON.

Function Parameters Returns IPC.StartServer (pipeName) boolean IPC.StopServer () — IPC.Send (message) — IPC.GetMessages () Table of strings ParseJSON (str) table ToJSON (obj) string

Protocol: Newline-delimited JSON. Pipe name "AoE_ML_Pipe" → \\.\pipe\AoE_ML_Pipe.

docs.title: Vector2, Vector3, Vector4
docs.text:
• Constructors: Vector2(x, y), Vector3(x, y, z), Vector4(x, y, z, w)
  • Properties: x, y, z, w
  • Methods: LengthSqr, Length, Normalize, Normalized, Dot, Cross, IsNearlyZero, Lerp, Distance
  • Operators: +, -, *, /, unary -

docs.title: Color
docs.text:
• Color.new(r, g, b) or Color.new(r, g, b, a) — 0–255
  • Color(r, g, b), Color(hexStr), Color.Parse, Color.HSV

docs.title: Game Types
docs.text: Type Source Description Complexity Object GetObjectsByType, GetObjectsByClass, GetObjectById Unit or building. Methods: GetId, GetPosition, GetHitpoints, IsAlive, GetUnitObjectType, GetClass, GetAttribute, etc. Low Player GetLocalPlayer, GetPlayerById Player. Methods: GetId, GetAttribute, GetUnitTypeCount, GetFact, CanAfford, GetObjectsByClass, GetTownCenters, etc. Low

docs.title: Strategic Component Types
docs.text: Type Constructor Description Complexity ResourceTracker ResourceTracker() Tracks trees, gold, stone, farms, etc. Methods: GetConvertibleLivestock, GetForage, GetFarms, GetTrees, GetGold, GetStone High VillagerOccupation VillagerOccupation(resourceTracker) Manages villager assignments. Methods: Update, GetVillagerCount, RequestVillagers, AssignVillagers High ConstructionPlacement ConstructionPlacement(villagerOccupation) Building placement. Methods: Update, TryBuildStructure, FindBestPosition, QueueBuildingRequest, ProcessBuildingRequests High

docs.title: 10. Enums
docs.text: Use as EnumName.VALUE.

docs.title: Game Enums (common values)
docs.text:
• PlayerType: NON_PLAYER, HUMAN, GAIA, BOT
  • VictoryCondition: STANDARD, CONQUEST, TIME_LIMIT, SCORE, CUSTOM
  • ResearchState: NOT_AVAILABLE, LOCKED, RESEARCHABLE, RESEARCHING, RESEARCHED
  • PlayerAttribute: FOOD, WOOD, STONE, GOLD, POP_SPACE_LEFT, POP_CURRENT, AGE
  • Age: DARK_AGE, FEUDAL_AGE, CASTLE_AGE, IMPERIAL_AGE
  • UnitCombatStance: AGGRESSIVE, DEFENSIVE, NO_ATTACK, STAND_GROUND
  • FactId: GAME_TIME, POPULATION_CAP, FOOD_AMOUNT, WOOD_AMOUNT, STONE_AMOUNT, GOLD_AMOUNT, POPULATION, etc.

docs.title: UnitClass, UnitObjectType, Technology
docs.text:
These enums have hundreds of values. Reference external tables:

  • UnitObjectType: https://airef.github.io/tables/objects.html
  • UnitClass: https://airef.github.io/parameters/parameters-details.html#ClassId
  • Technology: https://airef.github.io/tables/techs.html
  • ObjectData: https://airef.github.io/parameters/parameters-details.html#ObjectData

docs.title: Key (for keybinds)
docs.text: • Key.Add (numpad +), Key.F, Key.SPACE, Key.Escape, Key.Num0–Key.Num9, Key.F1–Key.F12, etc.

docs.title: 11. Limits
docs.text:
• require depth: Max 3 levels
  • Multiplayer: Disabled
  • Sandbox: No load, loadfile, dofile, module, collectgarbage
  • Game API: Only call from Init, Update, or Render
  • Performance: Avoid heavy work in Render (runs every frame)

docs.title: Minimal Module (Settings + Render)
docs.text:
-- my_module.main.lua
local logger = require("utils.logger")

function Load()
    Settings.AddColor("Villager Color", Color.new(0, 255, 0))
    Settings.AddKeybind("Increase Game Speed", Key.Add)
    logger.info("Module loaded")
end

function Init()
    ChatLocal("Hello from my module!")
end

function Update()
end

function Render()
    local key = Settings.GetKeybind("Increase Game Speed", Key.Add)
    if IsKeyPressed(key) then
        SetGameSpeedMultiplier(30)
    else
        SetGameSpeedMultiplier(1.5)
    end

    local color = Settings.GetColor("Villager Color", Color.new(0, 255, 0))
    local villagers = GetObjectsByClass(UnitClass.VILLAGER)
    for i = 1, #villagers do
        RenderObjectBoundsFilled(villagers[i], color)
    end
end

function End(hasWon)
    logger.info("Game ended: " .. (hasWon and "Victory!" or "Defeat!"))
end

docs.title: IPC Module (External Agent)
docs.text:
-- ipc_module.main.lua
local pipeName = "AoE_ML_Pipe"

function Init()
    IPC.StartServer(pipeName)
end

function Update()
    local messages = IPC.GetMessages()
    for _, msgStr in ipairs(messages) do
        local cmd = ParseJSON(msgStr)
        if cmd and cmd.action == "Scout" and cmd.target then
            -- UnitsMove(cmd.unitIds, Vector3(cmd.target.x, cmd.target.y, 0))
        end
    end

    local state = { time = GetGameTime(), playerCount = GetPlayerCount(), isAlive = true }
    IPC.Send(ToJSON(state))
end

function End()
    IPC.StopServer()
end

docs.title: Logger Submodule
docs.text:
-- utils/logger.lua
local logger = {}
function logger.info(message)
    Log("[Bot] " .. message)
end
return logger

docs.title: 13. Agent Instructions
docs.text:
When assisting users to build CONTROL modules:

  1. Always create the entry file as {moduleName}.main.lua inside modules/{moduleName}/.
  2. Use the exact function names and signatures from this document.
  3. Call game API only from Init, Update, or Render.
  4. Call Settings.Add* only from Load.
  5. Respect the require depth limit of 3.
  6. Reference https://airef.github.io for UnitObjectType, UnitClass, Technology IDs when the user needs specific unit/tech IDs.
  7. Suggest the full module structure including submodules when the user's request is complex.
  8. Remind users that multiplayer is disabled and the engine is single-player only.

docs.title: Game API — Commands
docs.text:
Commands perform actions in the game. Call them from Init, Update, or Render when the game is active.

Game must be running

Calling game API before the game starts logs an error. Move logic to Init, Update, or Render.

docs.title: Reference Table
docs.text: Function Parameters Description Complexity Log (message) Write to the log window Not complex SetGameSpeedMultiplier (multiplier) Set game speed (e.g. 1.5, 30) Not complex SetCameraPosition (position) Move camera to Vector2 position Not complex ChatLocal (message) Send message to local chat Not complex TrainUnit (trainSources, unitId, amount?) Train units from specified buildings Low UnitsTargetObject (units, target) Order units to attack/target an object Low UnitsBuildStructure (builders, structureId, position) Order builders to construct Low UnitsMove (units, position) Move units to world position Low EnableScouting () Enable auto-scouting; returns success Low ResearchTechnology (researchSources, technology) Research tech at specified buildings Low DeleteUnit (unit) Delete a unit Low DestroyBuilding (building) Destroy a building Low SetGatherPoint (buildings, targetPosition) Set gather point for buildings Low RingTownBell (building, isCallingIn) Ring town bell (call in / send out) Low SendBackToWork (building) Send garrisoned units back to work Low SendAllBackToWork (building) Send all garrisoned units back to work Low SetUnitStanceAutoScout (units) Set units to auto-scout stance Low SetUnitStancePatrol (units, targetPosition) Set units to patrol Low SetUnitStanceGuard (units, targetObject) Set units to guard object Low SetUnitStanceFollow (units, targetObject) Set units to follow object Low SetUnitStanceAttackMove (units, targetPosition) Set units to attack-move Low SetUnitStanceGarrison (units, targetObject) Garrison units in building Low SetUnitStanceUngarrison (sourceObjects, unit) Ungarrison unit from building Low SetUnitStanceSeekShelter (units) Set units to seek shelter Low SetUnitCombatStance (units, stance) Set combat stance (aggressive, defensive, etc.) Low

docs.title: TrainUnit
docs.text:
TrainUnit(trainSources, unitId, amount?)
  • trainSources: Table of UnitObjectType (building types that can train, e.g. Town Centers)
  • unitId: UnitObjectType of the unit to train
  • amount: Optional, defaults to 1
TrainUnit({UnitObjectType.TOWN_CENTER_FEUDAL_AGE}, UnitObjectType.VILLAGER_MALE, 1)

docs.title: EnableScouting
docs.text: Returns boolean — whether scouting was successfully enabled.

docs.title: Discord
docs.text:
Join the Discord server for:

  • Support — Ask questions about scripting and the engine
  • Sharing — Share your modules and scripts
  • Encryption — Request project encryption and packaging for .module distribution

docs.title: State
docs.text: The community is in early stages. Contributions, feedback, and shared modules are welcome.

docs.title: VS Code Plugin
docs.text: A Visual Studio Code plugin is planned to provide autocomplete and suggestions for CONTROL endpoints, making development easier.

docs.title: Config Location
docs.text: All CONTROL configuration and module data lives in a single folder.

docs.title: Path
docs.text:
%appdata%\CONTROL\AoE2Control\

On Windows, %appdata% typically resolves to C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming.

docs.title: Contents
docs.text: Item Description settings.ini Engine and module settings (AI enabled, module choice, update interval, per-module settings) imgui.ini ImGui layout (window positions, etc.) modules/ Folder for your Lua module projects

docs.title: Module Projects
docs.text:
Place your module projects inside modules/:

modules/
├── my_first_module/
│   ├── my_first_module.main.lua
│   └── utils/
│       └── logger.lua
└── ipc_test/
    └── ipc_test.main.lua

Depth limit: Subfolders are supported up to 3 levels for module discovery and require().

docs.title: What Is Distributed
docs.text: • CONTROL Launcher (EXE) — Loads the engine into the game

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Start Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition
  2. Run the CONTROL launcher
  3. Press START to attach the engine to the game

Once attached, the engine overlay appears and the config folder is created.

docs.title: Antivirus
docs.text: The engine may be flagged by antivirus due to techniques (process injection, memory access) that resemble some malware. Users may need to adjust Windows Defender or add an exclusion.

docs.title: Updates
docs.text: The engine may need updates after major AoE2DE patches. Check for new releases when the game updates. Join the Discord server for announcements and support.

docs.title: Enums
docs.text: CONTROL exposes game and engine enums as Lua tables. Use them as EnumName.VALUE.

docs.title: PlayerType
docs.text: Value Description NON_PLAYER 0 HUMAN 1 GAIA 2 BOT 3

docs.title: VictoryCondition
docs.text: Value Description STANDARD 0 CONQUEST 1 TIME_LIMIT 2 SCORE 3 CUSTOM 4

docs.title: ResearchState
docs.text: Value Description NOT_AVAILABLE -1 LOCKED 0 RESEARCHABLE 1 RESEARCHING 2 RESEARCHED 3

docs.title: PlayerAttribute
docs.text: Value Description FOOD 0 WOOD 1 STONE 2 GOLD 3 POP_SPACE_LEFT 4 POP_CURRENT 11 AGE 21

docs.title: Age
docs.text: Value Description DARK_AGE 0 FEUDAL_AGE 1 CASTLE_AGE 2 IMPERIAL_AGE 3

docs.title: UnitClass
docs.text:
See the community Objects Table (ClassId) for the full list of class IDs.

GetObjectsByClass(UnitClass.VILLAGER)

docs.title: UnitObjectType
docs.text:
See the community Objects Table for the full list of unit and building IDs.

TrainUnit({UnitObjectType.TOWN_CENTER_FEUDAL_AGE}, UnitObjectType.VILLAGER_MALE, 1)

docs.title: Technology
docs.text: See the community Technologies Table for the full list of technology IDs.

docs.title: UnitCombatStance
docs.text: Value Description AGGRESSIVE 0 DEFENSIVE 1 NO_ATTACK 2 STAND_GROUND 3

docs.title: FactId
docs.text:
Fact IDs for GetFact. Examples:

Value Description GAME_TIME 0 POPULATION_CAP 1 POPULATION_HEADROOM 2 FOOD_AMOUNT 5 WOOD_AMOUNT 6 STONE_AMOUNT 7 GOLD_AMOUNT 8 POPULATION 30 ... (see game data for full list) 
GetFact(FactId.POPULATION, 0)
GetFact(FactId.FOOD_AMOUNT, 0)

docs.title: ObjectData
docs.text: See the community Parameter Details (ObjectData) for the full list of object data field IDs.

docs.title: UrgencyLevel
docs.text: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH

docs.title: BuildingPosition
docs.text: TOWN_CENTER, AGGRESSIVE

docs.title: VillagerProfession
docs.text: WOOD, FOOD, STONE, GOLD

docs.title: PlacementDirection
docs.text: Used by ConstructionPlacement.

docs.title: BuildingRequestPriority
docs.text: Used by ConstructionPlacement.

docs.title: Key
docs.text:
Virtual key codes for keybinds (e.g. Key.Add for +, Key.F, Key.SPACE). Used with Settings.AddKeybind and IsKeyPressed.

Settings.AddKeybind("Hotkey", Key.Add)
if IsKeyPressed(Key.Add) then
    -- ...
end

docs.title: Game API — Facts
docs.text:
Facts read game state. Call them from Init, Update, or Render when the game is active.

Game must be running

Calling game API before the game starts logs an error. Move logic to Init, Update, or Render.

docs.title: Reference Table
docs.text: Function Parameters Returns Description Complexity GetFact (factId, parameter?) number Get fact value (population, resources, etc.) Low GetUnitTypeCount (unitId) number Count of unit type for local player Low GetAttribute (attribute) number Get player attribute (food, wood, etc.) Low CanAfford (unitId, isBuilding?) boolean Can local player afford unit/structure Low CanResearch (technology) boolean Can research technology Low IsTechnologyResearched (technology) boolean Is technology already researched Low GetObjectsByType (unitType) Table of Object Objects of specific type Low GetObjectsByTypes (unitTypes) Table of Object Objects matching any of the types Low GetObjectsByClass (unitClass) Table of Object Objects of class (e.g. VILLAGER) Low GetGameTime () number Current game time (seconds) Not complex GetLocalPlayer () Player Local player object Not complex GetPlayerById (id) Player Player by ID Low GetPlayerCount () number Number of players Not complex IsEnemyPlayer (player) boolean Is player an enemy Low GetObjectById (id) Object Object by ID Low GetVictoryCondition () VictoryCondition Current victory condition Low GetVictoryPlayer () Player? Winning player (if game ended) Low

docs.title: GetFact
docs.text:
GetFact(factId, parameter?)
  • factId: FactId enum (e.g. FactId.POPULATION, FactId.FOOD_AMOUNT)
  • parameter: Optional, often 0 for local player
local pop = GetFact(FactId.POPULATION, 0)
local food = GetFact(FactId.FOOD_AMOUNT, 0)

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through installing CONTROL and creating your first Lua module.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Start Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition — Ensure the game is running
  2. Run the CONTROL launcher
  3. Press START — Attach the engine to the game

Once attached, the engine overlay appears and the config folder is created.

docs.title: First Run
docs.text:
After the first run, the configuration folder is created at:

%appdata%\CONTROL\AoE2Control\

This folder contains:

  • settings.ini — Engine and module settings
  • imgui.ini — UI layout preferences
  • modules/ — Your Lua module projects

docs.title: First Script
docs.text:
Create a new module in the modules folder:

  1. Create a folder: modules\my_first_module\
  2. Create the entry file: my_first_module.main.lua
  3. Add the required callbacks:
function Load()
    -- Settings go here (add checkboxes, sliders, etc.)
end

function Init()
    -- Runs when game starts/resets
end

function Update()
    -- Runs every update tick (configurable interval)
end

function Render()
    -- Runs every frame — draw overlays here
end

function End(hasWon)
    -- Runs when game ends
end

All callbacks are optional — implement only what you need.

docs.title: Enable Your Module
docs.text:
1. Open the CONTROL interface (in-game overlay)
  2. Select your module from the Module dropdown
  3. Enable AI (toggle)
  4. Start or load a single-player game

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Example — A complete working module with Settings and Render
  • Lifecycle — When each callback runs
  • Module System — require, depth limits, encrypted modules

docs.title: Interface Options
docs.text: The CONTROL engine provides an in-game overlay with options for module selection and AI behavior.

docs.title: Module Selection
docs.text:
A dropdown lists all available modules. The engine searches recursively for:

  • *.main.lua — Plain Lua entry points
  • *.main.module — Encrypted precompiled modules

Search depth: Up to 3 levels inside modules/.

Example: modules/my_first_module/my_first_module.main.lua appears as my_first_module in the dropdown.

docs.title: AI Settings
docs.text:
Setting Description Default Enabled Toggle to run the selected module Off Module Dropdown to select which module runs; Open Folder opens the module directory — Update Interval Seconds between Update() calls 1.0 Sequential Actions When enabled, limits commands per tick On Auto Move Camera Move camera to command targets On Command Visualization Show visual feedback for commands On

The Update Interval is clamped to a minimum of 0.1 seconds in the ui and 0.01 seconds when manually editing the settings file.

docs.title: Log Window
docs.text: An option exists to show only script logs (default) — filtering out engine-internal messages. Use Log("message") in your scripts to write to this window.

docs.title: IPC API
docs.text: The IPC API lets external processes (e.g. Python ML agents) communicate with your Lua module via named pipes and JSON.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Function Parameters Returns Description IPC.StartServer (pipeName) boolean Start named pipe server IPC.StopServer () — Stop server IPC.Send (message) — Send string to client IPC.GetMessages () Table of strings Receive messages from client

docs.title: JSON Helpers
docs.text: Function Parameters Returns Description ParseJSON (str) table Parse JSON string to Lua table ToJSON (obj) string Serialize Lua table to JSON

docs.title: Lua Server Example (ipc_test)
docs.text:
local pipeName = "AoE_ML_Pipe"

function Init()
    Log("Starting IPC Server on pipe: " .. pipeName)
    IPC.StartServer(pipeName)
end

function Update()
    -- 1. Ingest commands from the ML Agent
    local messages = IPC.GetMessages()

    for _, msgStr in ipairs(messages) do
        local cmd = ParseJSON(msgStr)

        if cmd then
            Log("ML Command Received: " .. cmd.action)
            if cmd.action == "Scout" then
                if cmd.target and cmd.target.x and cmd.target.y then
                    -- UnitsMove(cmd.unitIds, Vector3(cmd.target.x, cmd.target.y, 0))
                end
            end
        end
    end

    -- 2. Build the current game state
    local gameState = {
        time = GetGameTime(),
        playerCount = GetPlayerCount(),
        isAlive = true
    }

    -- 3. Broadcast state to the ML Agent
    IPC.Send(ToJSON(gameState))
end

function End()
    Log("Shutting down IPC Server...")
    IPC.StopServer()
end

docs.title: Python Client Example
docs.text:
import win32pipe, win32file, pywintypes
import json
import time

PIPE_NAME = r"\\.\pipe\AoE_ML_Pipe"

def connect_to_engine():
    while True:
        try:
            handle = win32file.CreateFile(
                PIPE_NAME, win32file.GENERIC_READ | win32file.GENERIC_WRITE,
                0, None, win32file.OPEN_EXISTING, 0, None
            )
            return handle
        except pywintypes.error as e:
            if e.winerror in (2, 231):  # ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND or ERROR_PIPE_BUSY
                time.sleep(1)
            else:
                raise

def main():
    handle = connect_to_engine()
    try:
        while True:
            # Read state from engine
            result, data = win32file.ReadFile(handle, 4096)
            if result == 0:
                for msg in data.decode('utf-8').strip().split('\n'):
                    if msg:
                        state = json.loads(msg)
                        print(f"State -> Time: {state.get('time')}")

            # Send command to engine
            command = {
                "action": "Scout",
                "unitIds": [101],
                "target": {"x": 150.5, "y": 200.0}
            }
            win32file.WriteFile(handle, (json.dumps(command) + "\n").encode('utf-8'))

            time.sleep(0.1)
    finally:
        win32file.CloseHandle(handle)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text:
• Pipe name: Pass to StartServer (e.g. "AoE_ML_Pipe" → \\.\pipe\AoE_ML_Pipe)
  • Format: Newline-delimited JSON messages
  • Read: IPC.GetMessages() returns array of received strings
  • Write: IPC.Send(ToJSON(gameState)) sends one message

docs.title: Lifecycle
docs.text: The engine invokes your module through five optional callbacks. Implement only the ones you need.

docs.title: All Callbacks Optional
docs.text: Modules can omit any callback. For example, ipc_test has no Load because it uses no Settings.

docs.title: Load
docs.text:
When: Once when the module is selected/loaded.

Use for: Settings.Add* only. Register checkboxes, sliders, dropdowns, keybinds, colors, etc. The engine calls settings::Load() after Load() returns.

Do not: Call game API (game may not be running yet).

docs.title: Init
docs.text:
When: When the game is ready, world changed, module reloaded, or AI enabled.

Use for: One-time setup (e.g. IPC.StartServer, ChatLocal).

docs.title: Update
docs.text:
When: At a configurable interval (default 1.0s) while the game is running.

Use for: Main game logic — commands, decisions, state updates.

docs.title: Render
docs.text:
When: Every frame while the game is running.

Use for: Drawing overlays — shapes, text, minimap markers.

docs.title: End
docs.text:
When: Once when the game ends.

Parameters: hasWon (boolean) — whether the local player won.

Use for: Cleanup, logging, IPC shutdown (e.g. IPC.StopServer()).

docs.title: Timing Diagram
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    L[Load] --> I[Init]
    I --> U[Update]
    U --> R[Render]
    R --> U
    U --> E[End]
  • Load runs once when the module is selected.
  • Init runs when entering a game.
  • Update and Render run in a loop until the game ends.
  • End runs once when the game ends.

docs.title: Limits
docs.text: This page documents the engine's limits and constraints.

docs.title: Module Depth
docs.text:
• require: Maximum depth of 3. require("a.b.c.d") fails.
  • Module discovery: Recursive search for *.main.lua / *.main.module up to 3 levels inside modules/.

docs.title: Multiplayer
docs.text: Multiplayer is disabled. The engine does not support multiplayer. Use only in single-player.

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• Large projects may need optimization. The Lua interpreter handles scripts of varying size; scripters are responsible for performance.
  • Avoid heavy work in Render() — it runs every frame.

docs.title: Sandbox
docs.text:
The following Lua functions are removed from the environment:

  • load
  • loadfile
  • dofile
  • module
  • collectgarbage

docs.title: Game API Timing
docs.text:
Game API (commands, facts, render) must be called when the game is active. Calling before the game starts logs an error:

Lua error: Game API function called before the game started. Move this logic to Init() or Update().

Move game logic to Init, Update, or Render.

docs.title: Module System
docs.text: CONTROL uses a custom module system. Entry points are *.main.lua or *.main.module files.

docs.title: Entry Point
docs.text: Format Description {moduleName}.main.lua Plain Lua source {moduleName}.main.module Encrypted precompiled module

docs.title: Folder Convention
docs.text:
Use modules/{moduleName}/{moduleName}.main.lua:

modules/
└── my_first_module/
    └── my_first_module.main.lua

The folder name matches the module name. The engine searches recursively (depth 3) for entry files.

docs.title: Path Resolution
docs.text:
• require("utils.logger") → utils/logger.lua (relative to project root)
  • Dot notation becomes path separators

docs.title: Depth Limit
docs.text:
Maximum depth: 3. Paths with more than 3 / segments fail:

require("a.b.c.d")  -- fails: depth exceeds 3
require("a.b.c")    -- ok

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
• No directory traversal: .. is forbidden
  • Caching: Modules are cached per path; second require returns cached result
  • Sandbox: load, loadfile, dofile, module, collectgarbage are removed

docs.title: Example Module
docs.text:
-- utils/logger.lua
local logger = {}

function logger.info(message)
    Log("[Bot] " .. message)
end

return logger

Usage: local logger = require("utils.logger") then logger.info("Hello").

docs.title: Encrypted Modules
docs.text:
For distribution and code protection, use .module files:

  1. Compile your project with the CONTROL module compiler
  2. Contact via Discord for project encryption and packaging (invite link to be added)

Encrypted modules are loaded as .main.module instead of .main.lua.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text: This page shows a minimal working module based on my_first_module. It demonstrates Settings, Render, Init, End, and require.

docs.title: Module Structure
docs.text:
modules/
└── my_first_module/
    ├── my_first_module.main.lua   ← Entry point
    └── utils/
        └── logger.lua             ← Submodule

docs.title: Full Example
docs.text:
-- my_first_module.main.lua
local logger = require("utils.logger")

function Load() -- runs when module is selected
    Settings.AddColor("Villager Color", Color.new(0, 255, 0))
    Settings.AddKeybind("Increase Game Speed", Key.Add)

    logger.info("Module loaded")
end

function Init() -- runs when the game starts / resets
    ChatLocal("Hello from my first module!")
end

function Update() -- runs every update tick
end

function Render() -- runs every frame
    local toggleColorKey = Settings.GetKeybind("Increase Game Speed", Key.Add)
    if IsKeyPressed(toggleColorKey) then
        SetGameSpeedMultiplier(30)
    else
        SetGameSpeedMultiplier(1.5)
    end

    local villagerColor = Settings.GetColor("Villager Color", Color.new(0, 255, 0))

    local objects = GetObjectsByClass(UnitClass.VILLAGER)
    for i = 1, #objects do
        RenderObjectBoundsFilled(objects[i], villagerColor)
    end
end

function End(hasWon) -- runs when the game ends
    logger.info("Game ended: " .. (hasWon and "Victory!" or "Defeat!"))
end

docs.title: Logger Submodule
docs.text:
-- utils/logger.lua
local logger = {}

function logger.info(message)
    Log("[Bot] " .. message)
end

return logger

docs.title: What This Example Demonstrates
docs.text: Feature Usage require require("utils.logger") loads utils/logger.lua Settings.AddColor Defines a color picker in the module UI Settings.AddKeybind Defines a hotkey (default: +) Settings.GetKeybind / GetColor Read values in Render IsKeyPressed Check if key is held (for hotkey) GetObjectsByClass Get all villagers RenderObjectBoundsFilled Draw filled overlay on each villager SetGameSpeedMultiplier Change game speed ChatLocal Send message to chat End(hasWon) React to game end

docs.title: Module Folder Convention
docs.text: Use modules/{moduleName}/{moduleName}.main.lua — the folder name matches the module name. The engine searches recursively for *.main.lua or *.main.module files (up to depth 3).

docs.title: Render API Implementation
docs.text: This page shows practical usage patterns for the Render API.

docs.title: When to Use Each Space
docs.text: Space Use for Screen HUD, fixed UI, resource display, FPS counter World Unit highlights, building markers, circles around selected units Minimap Enemy positions, resource locations, strategic markers

docs.title: Example: Villager Overlay (my_first_module)
docs.text:
Draw filled bounds on each villager, with color from Settings:

function Render()
    local villagerColor = Settings.GetColor("Villager Color", Color.new(0, 255, 0))
    local objects = GetObjectsByClass(UnitClass.VILLAGER)

    for i = 1, #objects do
        RenderObjectBoundsFilled(objects[i], villagerColor)
    end
end

docs.title: Example: Custom HUD (Screen Space)
docs.text:
Resource display in a corner:

function Render()
    local player = GetLocalPlayer()
    if not player then return end

    local pos = Vector2(100, 50)
    local size = 14.0
    local color = Color.new(255, 255, 255)

    RenderText("Food: " .. tostring(GetAttribute(PlayerAttribute.FOOD)), pos, size, color, false, true)
    pos.y = pos.y + 20
    RenderText("Wood: " .. tostring(GetAttribute(PlayerAttribute.WOOD)), pos, size, color, false, true)
    pos.y = pos.y + 20
    RenderText("Gold: " .. tostring(GetAttribute(PlayerAttribute.GOLD)), pos, size, color, false, true)
end

docs.title: Example: World-Space Marker
docs.text:
Circle around a unit's position:

local unit = GetObjectById(someId)
if unit and unit:IsAlive() then
    local pos = unit:GetPosition()
    RenderWorldCircleFilled(pos, 2.0, Color.new(255, 0, 0, 80))
end

docs.title: Example: Minimap Indicators
docs.text:
Mark positions on the minimap:

-- Mark enemy positions
local players = {}
for i = 0, GetPlayerCount() - 1 do
    local p = GetPlayerById(i)
    if p and IsEnemyPlayer(p) then
        local townCenters = p:GetTownCenters()
        for _, tc in ipairs(townCenters) do
            if tc and tc:IsAlive() then
                RenderMinimapDot(tc:GetPosition(), 3, Color.new(255, 0, 0))
            end
        end
    end
end

docs.title: Render API
docs.text: The Render API draws overlays on top of the game. Use it in Render().

docs.title: Coordinate Spaces
docs.text: Space Use for Functions Screen HUD, fixed UI GetScreenSize, IsOnScreen, RenderText, RenderLine, RenderCircle, RenderRect World Map markers, unit highlights WorldToScreen, IsWorldPosOnScreen, RenderWorld, RenderObjectBounds Minimap Minimap indicators WorldToMinimap, RenderMinimap*

docs.title: Base (Screen Space)
docs.text: Function Parameters Description Complexity GetScreenSize () Returns Vector2 (width, height) Not complex IsOnScreen (position) Is Vector2 position on screen Not complex RenderText (text, position, size, color, center, border?) Draw text at screen position Low RenderLine (from, to, color, thickness) Draw line between two Vector2 points Low RenderCircle (position, radius, color, thickness, segments?) Draw circle outline Low RenderCircleFilled (position, radius, color, segments?) Draw filled circle Low RenderRect (from, to, color, rounding, roundingCornersFlags, thickness) Draw rectangle outline Low RenderRectFilled (from, to, color, rounding, roundingCornersFlags) Draw filled rectangle Low

docs.title: Game (World / Minimap)
docs.text: Function Parameters Description Complexity IsWorldPosOnScreen (worldPos) Is Vector3 world position visible Low WorldToScreen (worldPos) Convert Vector3 world position to Vector2 screen Medium WorldToMinimap (worldPos) Convert Vector3 world position to Vector2 minimap Medium GetZoom () Current camera zoom Low GetCameraPosition () Camera position as Vector2 Low RenderWorldLine (from, to, color, thickness?) Line in world space Low RenderWorldRect (worldPos, width, height, color, thickness?) Rectangle in world space Low RenderWorldRectFilled (worldPos, width, height, color) Filled rectangle in world space Low RenderWorldCircle (worldPos, radius, color, thickness?, segments?) Circle in world space Low RenderWorldCircleFilled (worldPos, radius, color, segments?) Filled circle in world space Low RenderWorldText (text, worldPos, size, color, center?, border?) Text at world position Low RenderObjectBounds (object, color, thickness?) Outline around object bounds Low RenderObjectBoundsFilled (object, color) Filled overlay on object bounds Low RenderMinimapDot (worldPos, radius, color) Dot on minimap Low RenderMinimapLine (worldPosFrom, worldPosTo, thickness, color) Line on minimap Low RenderMinimapRect (worldPos, width, height, color, thickness) Rectangle on minimap Low RenderMinimapRectFilled (worldPos, width, height, color) Filled rectangle on minimap Low

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
-- Villager overlay (from my_first_module)
local objects = GetObjectsByClass(UnitClass.VILLAGER)
for i = 1, #objects do
    RenderObjectBoundsFilled(objects[i], villagerColor)
end

RenderObjectBoundsFilled

Takes exactly two parameters: (object, color). Extra arguments are ignored.

docs.title: Settings API
docs.text: The Settings API lets modules add UI controls (checkboxes, sliders, dropdowns, keybinds, colors) to the engine interface. Settings are per-module.

docs.title: Add Functions (Load only)
docs.text:
Call these only from Load():

Function Parameters Description Settings.AddBool (key, default) Checkbox Settings.AddInt (key, default, min, max) Integer slider Settings.AddFloat (key, default, min, max) Float slider Settings.AddDropdown (key, default, options) Dropdown (options = table of strings) Settings.AddKeybind (key, defaultVkCode) Keybind (use Key.Add, Key.F, etc.) Settings.AddColor (key, defaultColor) Color picker Settings.AddTooltip (key, tooltip) Tooltip for a setting

docs.title: Get Functions (anywhere)
docs.text:
Call these from Load, Init, Update, or Render:

Function Parameters Returns Description Settings.GetBool (key, default?) boolean Get checkbox value Settings.GetInt (key, default?) number Get int value Settings.GetFloat (key, default?) number Get float value Settings.GetString (key, default?) string Get string/dropdown value Settings.GetKeybind (key, defaultVkCode?) number Get keybind VK code Settings.GetColor (key, defaultColor) Color Get color value

docs.title: IsKeyPressed
docs.text:
IsKeyPressed(vkCode)  -- returns boolean

Check if a key is currently pressed. Use with Settings.GetKeybind for hotkeys.

docs.title: Example (my_first_module)
docs.text:
function Load()
    Settings.AddColor("Villager Color", Color.new(0, 255, 0))
    Settings.AddKeybind("Increase Game Speed", Key.Add)
end

function Render()
    local toggleColorKey = Settings.GetKeybind("Increase Game Speed", Key.Add)
    if IsKeyPressed(toggleColorKey) then
        SetGameSpeedMultiplier(30)
    else
        SetGameSpeedMultiplier(1.5)
    end

    local villagerColor = Settings.GetColor("Villager Color", Color.new(0, 255, 0))
    -- use villagerColor for rendering
end

docs.title: Types
docs.text: CONTROL exposes several types for use in Lua scripts.

docs.title: Vector2
docs.text:
Property Type Description x, y number Components

Constructors: Vector2(), Vector2(x, y), Vector2(scalar)

Methods: LengthSqr, Length, Normalize, Normalized, Dot, Cross, IsNearlyZero, Lerp, Distance

Operators: +, -, *, /, unary -

docs.title: Vector3
docs.text:
Property Type Description x, y, z number Components

Constructors: Vector3(), Vector3(x, y, z), Vector3(scalar)

Methods: LengthSqr, Length, Normalize, Normalized, Dot, Cross, IsNearlyZero, Lerp, Distance

Operators: +, -, *, /, unary -

local pos = Vector3(100, 200, 0)
-- or Vector3.new(x, y, z) if available

docs.title: Vector4
docs.text:
Property Type Description x, y, z, w number Components

Constructors: Vector4(), Vector4(x, y, z, w), Vector4(scalar)

Methods: LengthSqr, Length, Normalize, Normalized, Dot, IsNearlyZero, Lerp, Distance

Operators: +, -, *, /, unary -

docs.title: Color
docs.text:
Constructors:

  • Color(r, g, b) — RGB, 0–255
  • Color(r, g, b, a) — RGBA, 0–255
  • Color(r, g, b, a) — float 0–1
  • Color(hexStr) — hex string

Static methods: Color.Parse, Color.HSV

Color.new(0, 255, 0)      -- green (used in my_first_module)
Color.new(0, 255, 0, 128) -- green with alpha

docs.title: Object
docs.text:
Returned by GetObjectsByType, GetObjectsByTypes, GetObjectsByClass, GetObjectById.

Method Returns Description Complexity GetId number Object ID Not complex GetObjectType ObjectType Type Not complex GetOwningPlayer Player Owner Not complex GetGarrisonObject Object? Garrison container Not complex GetTargetPosition Vector3 Target position Not complex GetTargetObject Object? Target object Not complex GetDirection number Facing direction Not complex IsVisible boolean Visible to local player Not complex IsAlive boolean Alive Not complex GetUnitObjectType UnitObjectType Unit type Not complex GetClass UnitClass Unit class Not complex GetAttribute number Attribute value Not complex GetObjectData number Object data field Not complex IsIdle boolean Idle Not complex IsMoving boolean Moving Not complex IsScouting boolean Scouting Not complex GetHitpoints number Current HP Not complex GetMaxHitpoints number Max HP Not complex GetPosition Vector3 World position Not complex

docs.title: Player
docs.text:
Returned by GetLocalPlayer, GetPlayerById, GetVictoryPlayer.

Method Returns Description Complexity GetId number Player ID Not complex GetPlayerType PlayerType Type (human, bot, etc.) Not complex GetPlayerObjects Table Objects owned Low GetCameraPosition Vector2 Camera position Not complex GetMouseHoveredObject Object? Hovered object Not complex GetSelectedObject Object? Selected object Not complex GetSelectedObjectCount number Selection count Not complex GetPlayerName string Name Not complex GetCivilizationId number Civ ID Not complex GetCivilizationName string Civ name Not complex HasWon boolean Won the game Not complex IsAlliedWith boolean Allied with player Not complex IsEnemyTo boolean Enemy to player Not complex GetColor Color Player color Not complex GetAttribute number Attribute Not complex GetUnitTypeCount number Unit type count Not complex GetFact number Fact value Not complex CanAfford boolean Can afford Low GetResearchState ResearchState Tech state Not complex CanAffordResearch boolean Can afford research Low CanResearch boolean Can research Low IsTechnologyResearched boolean Tech researched Not complex GetObjectsByTypes Table Objects by types Low GetObjectsByMostCommonType Table By most common type Low GetObjectsByClass Table Objects by class Low GetTownCenters Table Town centers Low

docs.title: Strategic Component Types
docs.text: Type Constructor Description Complexity ResourceTracker ResourceTracker() Tracks trees, gold, stone, farms, etc. Methods: GetConvertibleLivestock, GetOwnedLivestock, GetDeadLivestock, GetForage, GetFarms, GetTrees, GetGold, GetStone High VillagerOccupation VillagerOccupation(resourceTracker) Manages villager assignments. Methods: Update, GetVillagerCount, RequestVillagers, AssignVillagers, SetPriorities, ResetPriorities High ConstructionPlacement ConstructionPlacement(villagerOccupation) Building placement. Methods: Update, TryBuildStructure, FindBestPosition, QueueBuildingRequest, ProcessBuildingRequests High
